fresh_facts.claim: The Pentagon executed Operation Epic Fury, a sustained military campaign against Iranian capabilities lasting 38 days of major combat operations.
More than 10,000 missions were flown during Operation Epic Fury, including 62 bomber missions with 18 flying round-trip from the United States.
More than 13,000 targets were struck in combined operations during Operation Epic Fury.
Approximately 90% of Iranian weapons factories were attacked, including every facility producing Shahed one-way attack drones.
Nearly 80% of Iran's nuclear industrial base was targeted during Operation Epic Fury.
More than 20 naval production and fabrication facilities were damaged or destroyed during Operation Epic Fury.
Allied forces intercepted 1,700 ballistic missiles and one-way attack drones throughout Operation Epic Fury.
Iran accepted a ceasefire under overwhelming military pressure as of April 9, 2026.
The ceasefire agreement includes provisions allowing ships passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
Russian Security Council Deputy Chair Dmitry Medvedev claimed on April 9 that the Strait of Hormuz is closed again.
Pentagon officials stated the ceasefire is 'a pause' with the Joint Force remaining ready to resume combat operations if ordered.
A-10 Warthogs were deployed to hunt fast attack watercraft in the Strait of Hormuz during Operation Epic Fury.
US President Trump set a final deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning of consequences if unmet.
Fars News Agency reported Iran halting tanker passage after Israeli strikes on Lebanon.
Iran's war ceasefire with the US and Israel was fragile on day 40 as of April 10, 2026.
US Vice President Vance stated Iran would be 'dumb' to break the ceasefire over Lebanon.
EU foreign policy chief emphasized talks must address Iran's wider threats amid ceasefire negotiations.
Netanyahu suggested direct Lebanon talks and sought more Arab peace deals during ceasefire period.
UAE President and UK PM discussed Iranian attacks during ceasefire period.
Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure triggered 'violent' economic shocks with lingering oil and gas price impacts.
Saudi Arabia cut oil output by 600,000 barrels per day due to the Iran-US-Israel war.
Mohamed El-Erian highlighted eroded global checks and balances causing frequent economic shocks like Hormuz closure.
US embassy in Bahrain lifted shelter advisory amid tensions related to the Iran conflict.
CSIS analysis warned the ceasefire masks dangers including unresolved Iranian nuclear ambitions and Lebanon destabilization.
CSIS warned of risks that Houthis entering the war could lead to a Bab al-Mandab blockade.
US is to host Israel-Lebanon talks scheduled for the following week as of April 10, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Operation Epic Fury decimated Iranian military capabilities with 13,000+ targets struck across 38 days, destroying ~90% of weapons factories and ~80% of the nuclear industrial base, forcing Iran into a fragile ceasefire now deteriorating over Strait of Hormuz closure and conflicting interpretations of ceasefire terms. The regime faces simultaneous pressures from US threats to resume strikes, economic shocks from oil price volatility, regional isolation as Israel pursues separate Lebanon deals, and compounding military weakening.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Operation Epic Fury campaign concluded with 10,000+ missions flown, 13,000+ targets struck, 1,700 air defense interceptions, and catastrophic damage to Iranian weapons production, nuclear facilities, and naval capabilities, forcing Iran to accept ceasefire under overwhelming military pressure.
